item.title: Codelco Eyes $2 Billion Gain From Unifying Copper Mines in Chile
item.description: Codelco is seeking a combined $2 billion in cost savings and additional revenue by integrating the operations of three copper mines as the state-owned Chilean company tries to offset the impact of stagnating output and rising debt.

item.title: Anthropic on Pace for First Profitable Quarter as Revenue Surges
item.description: Anthropic PBC is on pace for its first profitable quarter after experiencing a surge in revenue driven by demand for its artificial intelligence software.

item.title: SpaceX Has Spent More Than $15 Billion on New Starship Rocket
item.description: SpaceX said it has sunk more than $15 billion into developing its massive Starship rocket, which is manufactured and launched out of SpaceX’s Starbase facility in South Texas.

item.title: Sergey Brin Funds Opposition to San Francisco ‘Overpaid’ CEO Tax
item.description: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is continuing to push deeper into California politics by donating to a group that opposes a proposed tax hike on businesses with highly-paid executives in San Francisco.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Filing Shows $4.28 Billion First-Quarter Net Loss
item.description: SpaceX facilities in Hawthorne, California. Photographer: Ethan Swope/Bloomberg

item.title: Musk’s SpaceX Files Publicly for Nasdaq IPO Under Symbol SPCX
item.description: SpaceX filed publicly for its initial public offering, moving Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and artificial intelligence company a step closer to delivering the world’s biggest-ever debut.

item.title: Moody’s Cuts Mexico Credit Rating to One Notch Above Junk
item.description: Moody’s Ratings cut Mexico’s credit score to the lowest tier of investment grade, citing the country’s weakening fiscal position and stoking concern Latin America’s second-largest economy is heading toward junk status.

item.title: Wells Fargo Hires Three Tech Investment Bankers from Rivals
item.description: Wells Fargo & Co. has hired a trio of bankers who focus on the technology sector from rivals Citigroup Inc., Bank of America Corp. and Deutsche Bank AG, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Brazilian President Moves to Tighten Rules on Big Tech, Social Media Content
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva issued two decrees tightening rules for social media and digital platforms in Latin America’s largest economy, escalating the government’s push to curb hate speech, misinformation and online crime.

item.title: US Seeks to Boost Energy Exports to India as Rubio Visits Delhi
item.description: The Trump administration wants to boost energy exports to India, the US ambassador in New Delhi said on Wednesday, ahead of a multi-day visit by Secretary of State Marco Rubio to the world’s most populous country.

item.title: Nvidia Gets Tepid Reaction to Forecast, Boosts Investor Rewards
item.description: Nvidia Corp., the world’s most valuable company, delivered a sales forecast that drew a tepid reaction from investors, even as revenue from data center operators continued to surge.

item.title: Fed Unveils Plan for New Payment Account for Clearing, Settling
item.description: The Federal Reserve unveiled a proposal to establish a new type of payment account, which some financial institutions could use for clearing and settling their payments.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Dealmaker Sees ‘Full Spectrum’ of M&A Ahead in AI
item.description: Morgan Stanley’s head of global technology M&A said acquisitions in artificial intelligence are coming in all sizes and in multiple industries as companies race to fill technology gaps across chips, power, networking and infrastructure.

item.title: Analysis of Nvidia’s First-Quarter Earnings
item.description: Nvidia during the Computex conference in Taipei. Photographer: Annabelle Chih/Bloomberg

item.title: Germany Coalition Set to Pursue 40% Stake in Tank-Maker KNDS
item.description: Germany’s ruling coalition decided to take a 40% stake in KNDS NV before the tankmaker’s planned initial public offering this summer, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Russian Hackers Who Breached SolarWinds Had Deep Access to Treasury Emails
item.description: New details about the 2020 incident.

item.title: Surf Air, SpaceX, xAI Staffers Line Up SPAC for Space Assets
item.description: A group of aviation and telecommunication experts, including former staffers from Surf Air Mobility, xAI, and SpaceX, are forming a blank check company that will seek to take a space company public, as Wall Street chases the industry.

item.title: Fed Minutes Show More Officials Warned of Rate-Hike Scenario
item.description: A majority of Federal Reserve officials warned the central bank would likely need to consider raising interest rates if inflation continued to run persistently above their 2% target.

item.title: PepsiCo to Raise Prices on Small Chip Bags Over Higher US Costs
item.description: PepsiCo Inc. is preparing to raise prices on some of its smaller bags of chips, according to people familiar with the matter, even after reining in the cost of its larger packages in response to consumer pushback.

item.title: The Race to Offer Compute Futures to Masses Has Already Started
item.description: Wall Street is racing to turn computing power into a tradable commodity with the first ETFs being filed even before the futures contracts they would track have started to trade.

item.title: Neil Dutta on How Economists Are Missing the Macro Impact of AI
item.description: There’s more to it than just GDP accounting

item.title: Airbnb Pushes Beyond Stays With Luggage Storage, Car Rentals, Hotels
item.description: Airbnb Inc. will let guests arrange luggage storage, airport pickups and car and equipment rentals, its latest effort to expand beyond short-term rentals.

item.title: Airbnb’s Chesky Says US ‘Misunderstanding’ Use of Chinese Open-Source AI Models
item.description: Airbnb Inc. Chief Executive Officer Brian Chesky defended his company’s use of Chinese artificial intelligence models, saying US lawmakers who worry Chinese firms can access Americans’ user data are “misunderstanding” the technology.

item.title: Carney Pitches Reluctant British Columbia on New Oil Pipeline to Fuel Asia
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney said people in British Columbia should see “substantial economic and financial benefits” from a proposed new oil pipeline to the province’s coast, which BC Premier David Eby opposes.

item.title: OpenAI Preparing for IPO Filing in Days or Weeks, Report Says
item.description: OpenAI is preparing to file for an initial public offering in the coming days or weeks, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing unnamed people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Treasury Sanctions Drug Traffickers For Laundering Profits via Crypto
item.description: The US Treasury Department announced sanctions against an operative for the Sinaloa Cartel who it says uses cryptocurrency to funnel drug-trafficking proceeds back into Mexico.

item.title: Low Dollar Volatility Forces FX Traders Into Carry, Value Bets
item.description: A sharp drop in volatility in the US dollar is pushing traders in the $9.5-trillion-a-day currency market toward carry wagers and bets on relative value in search of profits.

item.title: Bitcoin Grows More Dependent on Michael Saylor’s Buying Machine
item.description: For most of its history, Bitcoin’s price was driven by a sprawling cast of buyers: idealists, speculators, early adopters, and, more recently, institutional investors looking for a new portfolio hedge. Demand was fragmented and hard to predict. In 2026, it is neither.

item.title: A 220% Bond Rally Snaps as Venezuela Euphoria Gets Reality Check
item.description: To the investors who had snapped up Venezuela’s defaulted bonds at beaten-down prices, it was the moment they were waiting for: Acting President Delcy Rodriguez’s government announced that it was moving quickly to kick off negotiations aimed at restructuring its $170 billion pile of debt.

item.title: Putin Leaves Beijing With Little Progress on Key Gas Pipeline
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin left Beijing with no sign of a breakthrough on a natural gas pipeline project with the potential to reshape global energy flows and geopolitics.
